Reynisfjara je najznámejšia „čierna pláž“ na Islande.

Je to jedna z najikonickejších a najfotografovanejších lokalít krajiny.
Absolútne výnimočnou ju robí čierny lávový piesok , ktorý vznikol drvením a eróziou sopečnej horniny.
Bazaltové stĺpy sú dokonalé hexagonálne útvary pri úpätí útesov Reynisfjall.
Reynisdrangar – dramatické skalné „ihly“ trčiace z oceánu.
Silné vlny – tzv. „sneaker waves“, ktoré sú nebezpečné aj pri pokojnom počasí.
Pláž sa nachádza pri mestečku Vík í Mýrdal na južnom pobreží Islandu.
Upozorňujem, že vlny na Reynisfjare sú mimoriadne silné a nevyspytateľné.
Nikdy sa nepribližuj blízko k vode, je to dosť nebezpečné.Pri fotení tiež opatrne, zapozeráš sa do diaľky a o chvíľu stojíš vo vode.
Pláž sa často objavuje vo filmoch a seriáloch. Odporúčam vidieť s určite si aj spomeniete v ktorých filmoch ste ju mohli vidieť.
압도적인 파도 검은색 모래 마치 집어삼켜질것 같은 느낌입니다. 짐깐이지만 너무 멋있었네요.
Not to be missed

Reynisfjara plajı, İzlanda'nın en ünlü siyah kumlu plajıdır. Güney Sahili boyunca uzanan bu sinematik mekan, kül rengi kıyıları, yükselen bazalt sütunları ve kayalıklara çarpan güçlü dalgalarıyla ünlüdür. Olağanüstü görünümü ve dramatik atmosferi, Game of Thrones gibi fantastik film ve diziler için favori bir fon haline gelmiştir.
Kuş gözlemcileri ve fotoğrafçılar için bir cennet olan Reynisfjara, vahşi güçlerin kontrolü ele geçirdiği, kükreyen Atlantik Okyanusu'nun adayı şekillendiren volkanik tarihle buluştuğu bir yerdir. İzlanda doğasının özünü yansıtan, mutlaka ziyaret edilmesi gereken bir yerdir.
Reynisfjara siyah kumlu plajının bir diğer benzersiz özelliği de, kıyı şeridini binlerce yıl boyunca şekillendiren doğal erozyonun bir sonucu olan, kıyıda ve açıkta bulunan yüksek bazalt oluşumlarıdır. Efsanelerle dolu bu uçurumlar, Mayıs'tan Ağustos'a kadar Güney Sahili'nde yuvalayan çeşitli İzlanda kuş türlerine ev sahipliği yapar.
Sayısız doğal güzellikle çevrili Reynisfjara, Güney İzlanda'nın diğer turistik yerlerine mükemmel bir geçiş noktasıdır. Bu volkanik plajı keşfettikten sonra , yakınlardaki Vík köyüne, Skógafoss'a, Seljalandsfoss şelalelerine ve Fjaðrárgljúfur kanyonuna yaklaşık bir saatlik sürüş mesafesinde ulaşabilirsiniz.
Reynisfjara Plajı, başkente yaklaşık 180 km (112 mil) uzaklıktadır. Reykjavik'ten Reynisfjara'ya gitmek için, asfalt Çevre Yolu'nu saat yönünün tersine yaklaşık 2,5 saat boyunca Vík köyü yönünde takip edin. 1. Rota genellikle yıl boyunca iyi durumdadır, ancak kışın yoğun kar yağışı nedeniyle yol almak daha zor olabilir. Vik'ten Reynisfjara'ya yolculuk sadece 12 dakika sürer.

review: Quirky themed bar that mainly serves burgers and white russians. 🤣 We went between 1 and 2 in the afternoon. The food was good, but pricey. ~$30 USD for a burger and fries. The drinks were good as well. Atmosphere is quirky and a bit broken up into a front, middle, and back. We were told to sit anywhere, so we sat in the back, then waited about 15 minutes for a waiter to ask about drinks. Now, while we weren't in a rush, we definitely felt like our waiter could not have cared less about doing more than the bare minimum. So much so, that we never saw him again after we received our food. We even ordered another round of drinks, but only did so when the bartender from the front of the restaurant walked by and asked. She cleared our table and eventually was the one who brought our check. As we walked out, our original waiter was just leaning against the bar. It wasn't enough to ruin our time there, but it's amazing how 1 person's attitude can impact an otherwise good experience.

We really enjoyed Lebowski Bar, it’s much bigger inside than it looks and has a great atmosphere. We went for a few drinks and ended up watching ice hockey and Premier League football while having an incredible Guinness burger and fries.

There are plenty of seating options (booths, tables, bar seating) and lots of screens throughout, so it’s great for groups of all sizes and even families. The staff were friendly, service was good, and we even met some locals while we were there.

Definitely recommend if you’re in Reykjavík.

We also popped in to watch some of the Men's Olympic Ice Hockey final. The bar was packed and the atmosphere was great!!

place_name: Harpa Concert Hall and Conference Centre
review:
My favourite place in Reykjavík!
Harpa is a unique building that looks like a huge iceberg rising from the harbour. It was designed by Henning Larsen Architects in collaboration with Icelandic-Danish artist Olafur Eliasson. The design was inspired by Iceland's hexagonal basalt columns, created by ancient volcanic eruptions. Its glass façade reflects the sea and the sky, changing colour with the light and the weather, so the building always looks different.
Harpa is the home of the Iceland Symphony Orchestra, and after visiting it, I now dream of coming back to Reykjavík just to attend a concert.
Inside, you'll also find the Lava Show, several designer shops, cafés, and, most importantly, an unforgettable view of the bay. No photograph can truly capture its beauty.
A place you simply must visit!

review: Már csak látni is valami hihetetlen : mintha egy különleges kristály lenne felnagyítva. Belülről is fantasztikus a néhol dupla üvegbúra, és nagyon ötletesen van felépítve: kívülről csak a nyilvános tereket látni, és a belső részben vannak a koncerttermek. A parkolója zseni : Parka alkalmazás megléte esetén meg sem kell nyitni, automatikusan kezeli a rendszer a ki - és behajtást. Az alsó részen pedig a nagyon reklámozott, 9' hosszú 4D-s lávafilm tekinthető meg...

Bardzo ciekawe, unikatowe muzeum. Dla osób lubiących mniej typowe miejsca i z dystansem - patrz sekcja pamiątek. Dużo eksponatów i jeszcze więcej ciekawostek na ich temat. Warte odwiedzenia.

What a museum of penises, who would go there? Everyone should LOL. If you are not interested in seeing penis then go for the coffee and humor. The coffee was great and the odd facts posted along with the shirts for purchase would have made this place great on their own.
Now about the penises.... You have all the different animal ones, then you have a large collection of human casts, and a real one (or 2). This place was very interesting and our kids had a hoot.
Highly recommended for all ages. (don't be prudes)

Our excursion got cancelled so we ended up walking around and found this, really good, informative and entertaining place which is suitable for all the family, nice cafe and gift shop too, was a good way to spend an hour or so and get out the cold, the nice touch is you can get beer oedrinks at the bar at the start and take it around with you.
c 'est surtout l'originalité qui fait l'intérêt. J'y aurais fait une salle plus médicale avec vasectomie ou autre fonctionnement du dit engin.

It takes about 45 minutes to see everything and it was so much fun. Never saw so much penises in one day.

Ben bir Müslümanım ve Bana Garip Geldi.Biyoloji ve Anatomi ile İlgilenen insanlar için harika ama bana sapıkça geldi.Her hayvanın hatta her insanın şeyini görmek İslam dininde yasak.Ama Diğer dinleri bilemem.Buraya 3 yıldız veriyorum.Bu benim fikrim.Aslında buraya hiç gelmedim ama fotoğraflarından garip gözüküyor.

Wer auf Phallusse aller Art steht, ist hier genau richtig. Neben dem besten Stück kann man zu den meisten Exemplaren auch noch etwas über das Lebewesen erfahren.

Wenn bekannt gibt es auch eine Angabe zur Durchschnittspenisgröße der Art.

Besonders interessant fand ich auch die kurze VR Show (in Kooperation mit Fly Ober Iceland) wo man auf eine kurze Entdeckungsreise über Island mitgenommen wird und sämtliche Orte, die phallusähnlich anmuten, gezeigt bekommt.

Ein Besuch lohnt sich definitiv.

review:

It’s possible to spend many hours in this museum if you read everything. It gets to be a bit long and monotonous towards the end. I don’t recall any benches where you could stop for a rest.

Is it appropriate for children? Maybe. Scientifically, absolutely! It depends on your parenting style.

Seljalandsfoss is one of Iceland's most iconic waterfalls, and what makes it truly special is that you can walk behind the waterfall itself. Seeing it from both the front and the back offers a completely different perspective.

We walked along the path behind the waterfall, and it was an unforgettable experience. If you plan to do the same, I highly recommend wearing a waterproof jacket and, if possible, waterproof shoes, as you'll get quite wet from the spray.

If you're visiting as part of a guided tour, make sure to manage your time carefully. Walking behind the waterfall, taking photos, and exploring the area can easily take longer than expected. There's also another beautiful waterfall within walking distance, so if you have enough time, it's well worth visiting that as well. It was one of the natural highlights of our trip to Iceland.

We were there mid July at 9:30 pm, there was not much crowd. God prepared you will get completely soaked if you go behind the falls, but it’s beautiful and worth getting soaked :) it was cold and windy too. There are few other falls smaller than this if you walk further. Must see its beautiful 😇

Seljalandsfoss was one of the most unforgettable waterfalls we visited in Iceland. The scenery surrounding the falls is absolutely stunning, and feeling the mist while taking in the views made it even more memorable. It's a must-see destination that truly showcases Iceland's natural beauty.

Seljalandsfoss is a stunning waterfall and one of the most memorable sights in Iceland. 冰島的間歇噴泉（Geyser），地下水被地熱加熱至沸點以上，當壓力達到臨界點，水蒸氣便會瞬間將大量沸水強行推擠出地表，形成高聳的水柱。即使正值夏日，氣候依然陰晴不定。當天是雨勢不斷，四周又瀰漫著硫磺味的白霧熱氣。

在又濕又冷的風雨中等待，雙手早已凍得麻木僵硬。然而，當幾十公尺高的熱水柱伴隨聲響衝天而起時，沸騰的熱氣與氣勢，卻無法抹去身上的寒意，成為旅途中最深刻的體驗。

Geysir Geothermal Area is a fascinating place to visit and one of Iceland’s most famous natural attractions. We watched the main geyser erupt approximately every 7–9 minutes, and each eruption was quite impressive to see in person. The area is also home to several other hot springs, where you can see the water bubbling and boiling, even if they don’t erupt like the geyser. Across the road, there is parking (for a fee) as well as visitor facilities where you can enjoy a coffee, take a break, and browse for souvenirs and local products.

Absolutely Stunning – A Must-See in Iceland!
​Gullfoss is an absolute masterpiece of nature. Standing on the viewing platforms and watching the sheer volume of water plunge down into the rugged canyon is an unforgettable experience. The raw power and scale of the falls are completely mesmerizing, and if you get lucky with a bit of sunshine, the rainbows cutting through the mist are spectacular.
​It is incredibly well-maintained with clear paths that let you experience the falls from different heights and perspectives. Whether you are viewing it from the top overlook or getting closer to feel the mist on your face, it completely takes your breath away.
​An absolute highlight of our time here—I loved every single second of it!
​Tip for other travelers: Wear waterproof gear or a good rain jacket if you plan to take the path closer to the water, as the spray will get you!

Reykjavík was one of the most charming cities we visited, with a perfect blend of colorful local culture, unique architecture, and stunning natural surroundings. We loved walking along the famous Rainbow Street, admiring the beautiful local buildings, and visiting the capital area, where the city’s history and modern character come together. One of our favorite surprises was seeing so many friendly cats throughout the city, adding even more charm to the streets. What stood out most was how incredibly clean, peaceful, and welcoming Reykjavík felt. Exploring the city on foot gave us a true appreciation for its beauty, personality, and laid-back atmosphere.

冰島雷克雅維克極具代表性的景點——彩虹街（Skólavörðustígur）。
這裡有幾個有趣的亮點和背景故事：
🌈 街區特色與背景
平權與包容的象徵：這條彩虹步道最初是在 2015 年為了當地的「雷克雅維克同志遊行（Reykjavík Pride）」而由志工與市民合力畫上的。因為大受歡迎，後來轉為永久性的景觀，象徵著冰島對多元性別與人權的包容與支持。
通往地標的朝聖之路：如果你沿著彩虹街往畫面的頂端（斜坡上方）望去，盡頭連接的就是雷克雅維克最著名的大教堂——哈爾格林姆教堂（Hallgrímskirkja）。這裡也是拍攝教堂遠景的經典絕佳角度。

這條街漆上了鮮豔的紅、橙、黃、綠、藍、紫，即便在冰島常見的陰天裡，也為整座城市增添了非常活潑且溫暖 urban 氣息，是來到雷克雅維克絕對不能錯過的打卡點！

If you want to experience a place that feels less like planet Earth and more like a scene from a dark fantasy epic, Reynisfjara is absolute magic.
​It is an understatement to just call it a black sand beach; it’s a jaw-dropping spectacle where the raw power of nature is on full display. The true highlight is standing on the shore and watching the roaring Atlantic waters violently clash from two different directions. It creates this hypnotic, chaotic dance of white foam against the pitch-black sand that you honestly can’t find anywhere else in the world.
​The View: Look to the horizon, and you're treated to a panoramic masterpiece. On one side, the legendary basalt sea stacks rise from the ocean like jagged frozen giants. On the other, the dramatic cliffs of Dyrhólaey stretch out into the sea, crowned by its iconic, lonely lighthouse watching over the coast.
​The contrast of the dark sand, the turquoise-rimmed waves, and the sweeping cliffs makes it a one-of-a-kind view that stays with you long after you leave. It’s wild, it’s moody, and it’s undeniably unforgettable. A total must-see!
​Tip: Just make sure to keep a respectful distance from the water—those sneaker waves are as real and powerful as the view itself!

Skógafoss was one of the most impressive waterfalls I saw in Iceland. You can hear the power of the water even before reaching it, and being able to walk right up to the falls makes the experience even more dramatic.

We got quite close, and within minutes we were covered in mist, so I definitely recommend bringing a waterproof jacket or raincoat. If you visit on a sunny day, there's a good chance you'll see a beautiful rainbow forming in front of the waterfall, making it an incredible spot for photos.

You can also climb the staircase beside the waterfall for a different viewpoint. However, if you're visiting as part of a guided tour, be sure to manage your time carefully. Visiting the base of the waterfall and climbing all the way up and back down can take longer than you might expect. On our tour, a few people had to turn back before reaching the top because they were running out of time. If your schedule is tight, it's worth deciding in advance how you'd like to spend your visit.

As a tour guide at GlaciGo, I have visited Diamond Beach countless times with guests from all over the world, and it still amazes me every single visit.

In my opinion, Diamond Beach is one of the most extraordinary places in Iceland. Every day is different because the icebergs that drift from Jökulsárlón Glacier Lagoon create a completely new landscape. Some pieces of ice are crystal clear, some are deep blue, and others are shaped by nature into incredible sculptures. Watching these ancient blocks of glacier ice resting on the black volcanic sand is an unforgettable experience.

What makes this place even more special is that no two visits are ever the same. The light, the waves, the weather, and the ice are constantly changing, making every visit unique. Whether you are a photographer, a nature lover, or simply someone who appreciates beautiful landscapes, Diamond Beach is a place that belongs on your bucket list.

As a tour guide at GlaciGo, I always look forward to bringing my guests here because I know it will be one of the highlights of their journey through Iceland. Every time I see the smiles on their faces, I’m reminded why this is one of my favorite places in the country. If you’re visiting Iceland, don’t miss the chance to experience the magic of Diamond Beach.

Globally unique and spectacular. There are beautiful places in the world, and then there are places that leave a permanent mark on your soul. Diamond Beach in Iceland is one of those rare places.

Standing on the black volcanic sand while crystal-clear icebergs, polished by the sea, sparkle like scattered diamonds is an experience that's difficult to put into words. Every wave rearranges nature's artwork, ensuring that no two visits are ever the same. The contrast between the deep black sand and the brilliant blue and crystal-white ice creates a landscape that feels almost otherworldly.

What makes Diamond Beach so special isn't just its incredible beauty—it's the reminder that even the most breathtaking things are temporary. The ice that has traveled for centuries from ancient glaciers eventually reaches the ocean, where it slowly melts away. Watching this quiet cycle of nature is both humbling and inspiring.

The silence is profound, broken only by crashing waves and the gentle crackling of melting ice. It's the kind of place that encourages you to slow down, breathe deeply, and appreciate the present moment. Whether you're a photographer chasing the perfect sunrise, a traveler searching for adventure, or simply someone looking for a moment of peace, Diamond Beach delivers something unforgettable.

Photos can capture the scenery, but they can never fully capture the feeling of standing there. It's a destination that reminds us that the world's greatest masterpieces aren't built by people—they're created by nature, one moment at a time.

If Iceland is on your bucket list, make Diamond Beach a priority. You won't just leave with incredible photographs—you'll leave with a renewed appreciation for the beauty, power, and fleeting moments that make life extraordinary.

Perlan is one of the most informative and engaging museums we visited in Iceland. We especially enjoyed learning about volcanoes, experiencing the Northern Lights show, and walking through the realistic ice cave. You can easily spend 2–3 hours here, and it’s a great experience for visitors of all ages.

Our 10 year old was captivated by this visit to an Ice Cave and the inside of a volcano. We also enjoyed the periodic eruptions of the indoor geysir. The revolving restaurant at the top was a delightful surprise as well as a lovely meal!

response_of_owner:
Dear Réka,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback with us.

We're pleased to hear you enjoyed the exhibitions, found the information useful, and that you had a great coffee in our café. We also appreciate your honest comments about your visit.

We understand that everyone has a different way of exploring museums, and many visitors prefer to discover exhibitions at their own pace. While most of Perlan can be explored freely, some of our immersive experiences — such as Áróra Northern Lights and Into the Volcano —operate on scheduled show times to ensure the best possible experience for all guests.

After taking part in these guided experiences, visitors are welcome to return and revisit any exhibition or show they have already experienced, allowing them to explore at their own pace, spend more time where they wish, or enjoy their favourite experience again during the same visit.

Thank you again for your visit and for sharing your perspective. We hope to welcome you back to Perlan in the future.

Best regards, 
Eva

review:
Sehr spannendes Naturkundemuseum, um mehr über Island und Gletscher und Vulkane zu lernen.

Ein besonderes Highlight ist der nachgebaute Gletscher mitten im Museum, ein Eistunnel, für den es sich lohnt, einen Pulli anzuziehen. Zur Not kann man auch vom Museum kostenfrei Ponchos ausleihen.

Das Museum ist in mehrere Stationen aufgeteilt. Ohne Wartezeit konnten wir im ersten Raum schon was über Vulkane im allgemeinen lernen. Beim Ticket kauf wurde uns dann ein Zeitslot zugewiesen, zudem wir einen Kinosaal betreten haben, wo ein Kurzfilm zu einem Vulkan(ausbruch) gezeigt wurde.

Anschließend konnten wir "live" dabei aein, wie sich die heiße Magma den weg nach oben bahnt um letzten Endes auszubrechen. Dazu wurde ein runder Kinosaal errichtet, ähnlich wie "The Sphere" in Las Vegas.

Nachdem wir einiges über Lava und Vulkane gelernt haben, ging es in den zu Beginn angesprochenen Gletschertunnel.
Anschließend gab es einen großen Raum, wo wir dann viel über unterschiedliche Gletscherarten gelernt haben. Außerdem wurden auch sämtliche Gletscher Islands zu den jeweiligen Gletscherformen eingeordnet.

Nach dem Lehrreichen Aufenthalt im Museum kann man sich im drehenden Restaurant/Bar erholen oder auf der Außenplattform einen Blick über die Landschaft, zum nationalen Stadtflughafen oder zur Stadt genießen.

response_of_owner:
Dear Rob, 

Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are sorry that you were unable to visit Perlan during your time in Reykjavík.

Advance booking is not generally required to visit Perlan, and guests are very welcome to purchase tickets upon arrival when availability allows. However, as one of the most popular attractions in Reykjavík and Iceland, certain days and time slots can sell out - particularly during the busy summer season.

For this reason, we strongly encourage guests to book through our official website in advance. This guarantees entry at the preferred time and also offers the best available ticket price.

Perlan is Iceland’s largest nature museum, offering immersive experiences dedicated to the country’s extraordinary natural wonders. These include a real indoor ice cave, the Áróra Northern Lights planetarium, glacier and volcanic exhibitions, and our newest experience, Into the Volcano.

We appreciate your comment and understand the disappointment of arriving when no suitable time slots remained. 

We hope you will have another opportunity to visit and experience everything Perlan has to offer.

Kind regards, 
Eva

review: Perlan completely blew me away  I honestly wasn't expecting much from a museum inside a giant dome on a hill, but it turned out to be one of the highlights of my entire Iceland trip. The natural history exhibits are beautifully done, but what really stopped me in my tracks were the volcano and glacier films. The immersive screen experience of a volcanic eruption felt almost uncomfortably real  I could practically feel the ground shaking. The glacier section was equally impressive, walking through ice formations while learning about how rapidly they're disappearing. It's one of those rare places where education and spectacle genuinely come together without feeling forced. The views from the observation deck over Reykjavik are worth the visit alone. But honestly, don't skip this place.
response_of_owner:

review:
We visited FlyOver Iceland with our family, and it was a fantastic experience!
It wasn’t crowded, the staff were friendly and attentive, and everything went smoothly from start to finish.

If you’re visiting Iceland and want to experience the country’s incredible natural beauty, I highly recommend FlyOver Iceland. It’s a great way to see some of Iceland’s most breathtaking landscapes, especially if you don’t have enough time to drive around the island.

Definitely worth the visit! 🇮🇸

💡One thing to keep in mind: the ride is quite exciting and can give you an adrenaline rush. If you’re visiting with family members or friends who have high blood pressure or aren’t comfortable with thrill rides, it’s worth considering whether this experience is right for them.
Definitely worth the visit! 🇮🇸

review:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

FlyOver Iceland was one of the highlights of our trip to Reykjavik and an experience the whole family loved.

The ride makes you feel as if you’re truly flying over Iceland’s breathtaking landscapes, from waterfalls and glaciers to volcanoes and black sand beaches. The visuals are spectacular, and the motion, wind, mist, and other special effects make the experience incredibly immersive.

Everything was well organized, the staff were friendly, and the quality of the attraction exceeded our expectations.

If you’re visiting Reykjavik, this is a must-do attraction. It’s perfect for families, couples, and anyone wanting to experience the beauty of Iceland from a unique perspective. Highly recommended!

review:
I would not recommend purchasing the Premium Experience. It is not worth the extra 2,300 ISK, as the advertised benefits, including premium seating and priority boarding, are not guaranteed due to poor organisation.

We loved our first visit to FlyOver Iceland in winter and returned this summer because we enjoyed it so much. The new summer film was fantastic and a great addition. Unfortunately, the new Premium Experience took away from what was otherwise an excellent attraction. Despite telling staff we had Premium Experience tickets, other guests pushed ahead of us and we were seated near the back. Although there was a premium boarding sign, there were no verbal announcements or staff directing premium guests, making the process confusing.

The complimentary drink and digital photos did not justify the extra cost. While some staff were understanding of our concerns, others were dismissive and blamed other customers instead of acknowledging the lack of organisation and poor communication within the staff.

The ride itself is still amazing, and we would happily recommend the attraction itself. However, until the premium benefits are properly managed and consistently delivered, I cannot recommend paying extra for the Premium Experience.
response_of_owner:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback, and we're truly sorry to hear that your Premium Experience did not meet your expectations.

We're delighted to hear that you enjoyed our new summer film and that you chose to visit us again after your first experience. However, it's disappointing to learn that the Premium Experience fell short due to the boarding process and the communication you received from our team. This is certainly not the experience we want our guests to have, and we sincerely apologize for the frustration it caused.

We would very much appreciate the opportunity to make this right. If you could please email us at info@flyovericeland.is with your booking details, our team would be happy to look into what happened and see how we can make it up to you.

Thank you again for your honest feedback. It helps us improve, and we hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back for the premium experience you were expecting.

Kind regards,
FlyOver Iceland Team

Þingvellir National Park was one of the most fascinating places we visited in Iceland, combining incredible natural beauty with deep historical significance. Walking through the dramatic landscape, we were able to see the meeting point of the North American and Eurasian tectonic plates, along with beautiful views of mountains, lava fields, and clear streams. Knowing that this area was also the site of Iceland’s historic Alþingi parliament made the experience even more meaningful. It was a place where geology, history, and breathtaking scenery came together in a truly unforgettable way.

This is *the* place to visit if you're visiting Iceland -  it's a spectacular rift valley 5 or 10km wide with most attractions on the western side - it's the site of the Icelandic parliament and summer residence. Also geologically spectacular, with deep clear water-filled chasms you can dive in, eg. at Silfra. Large campsites with plenty of availability. Don't miss this.

辛格維利爾國家公園（Þingvellir）是冰島唯一的世遺國家公園。震撼的是其地質與地形：橫跨北美與歐亞大陸板塊的交界，板塊每年以約2公分的速度拉開，形成巨大的裂谷與斷層（如著名的 Almannagjá 裂縫）。地表覆蓋著古老的玄武岩熔岩流，極具粗獷的荒涼美。儘管環境嚴酷，仍孕育獨特的低溫植物。熔岩荒原上鋪滿了厚厚的針葉泥炭蘚（Moss）與地衣，夏季時，矮樺樹、柳樹以及耐寒的野花（如石竹和北極罌粟）會在岩縫間點綴出微小的生機。雖是七月盛夏，冰島的冷雨卻伴隨寒風將體感溫度拉低至將近攝氏5度。我穿著防水羽絨衣走進斷崖深處，細雨將黑色的玄武岩石壁澆灌得發亮，原本翠綠的苔原在陰天下顯得格外濃郁。雖然雙手凍得發抖，走在兩大板塊的夾縫中，欣賞這座史詩級的地質奇景。

Jökulsárlón was even more breathtaking than I had imagined from photos and videos. Massive icebergs broken away from the glacier drift slowly across the lagoon, each one glowing in shades of white, blue, and crystal-clear ice. The scenery feels almost unreal, like standing on another planet shaped entirely by nature.

What made the experience unforgettable was the silence and scale of the landscape. You can hear the wind, the water, and the subtle cracking of the ice as it moves. The view constantly changes depending on the light and weather, making it impossible to look away. Every moment feels different.

Nearby, Diamond Beach was equally stunning. Seeing chunks of sparkling ice scattered across the black sand beach created one of the most beautiful contrasts I’ve ever seen.

This is not just a sightseeing spot — it’s a place you truly experience with all your senses. The raw power and beauty of Icelandic nature leave a lasting impression that stays with you long after you leave. It’s honestly one of those places everyone should see at least once in their lifetime.
Very very unique place. Have to see by yourself

I don’t know how fast this can change, and in summer obviously not booking might be a bit risky, but we were very happy with our strategy.

La vastità e l'imponenza del Vatnajökull sullo sfondo incutono un profondo senso di rispetto e ammirazione; è impossibile restare indifferenti di fronte alla potenza della natura che si manifesta qui.

review:
If I had to choose between blue lagoon and sky lagoon, sky lagoon. If you have kids, blue lagoon will be a better experience as it has more room for them to be kids. Sky Lagoon feels more of a luxury adult experience! If your kid is older, late teens, they might be more interested in Sky Lagoon. Either way, I went to both and while I had more rain during Blue Lagoon visit, thoroughly enjoyed Sky Lagoon and doing the ritual more. I had an 8:30p reso and stayed up until 10:30 where everyone gets out of the lagoon. This is summer, July 2026.

Hope this helps!

response_of_owner: Hi there Apostolos Karafyllidis, thank you for taking the time to review your visit to Sky Lagoon. We are so happy to see how much you enjoyed it and hope to welcome you again one day!

review: Loved it and the staff was wonderful.

Sky Lagoon is built for a higher-end, rest and relaxation experience. The water is not as naturally cool looking like thr one at Blue Lagoon but the infinity pool and their 7 step ritual will compensate for that. If you're looking for a more relaxed experience (no kids under 12) choose this one. Honestly one should try both this and Blue Lagoon as they offer different vibes. We loved our private shower and locker room and drinks were great too. I have to say that because the area is much smaller than the Blue Lagoon, it fills up much faster so maybe try either early morning of late afternoon checkin to see less crowds.

Visiting Sky Lagoon was a highlight of our trip to Iceland, not just Reykjavik.

The staff was very friendly, helpful, and attentive.

The facilities were immaculate.

The atmosphere, unforgettable.

If you are looking for an unforgettable experience around Reykjavik, definitely visit. And when you do, grab a few drinks, grab a bite (the charcuterie was amazing) and you MUST do the seven step ritual.

Had a very nice and relaxing experience at Sky Lagoon. Went with my wife and got the Sér package. While the Skjól ritual feels a little forced, we both enjoyed the different components and the ritual is entirely optional. The lagoon itself was warm and pleasant with variations in temperature, underwater benches, and a warm waterfall on one end. We found the staff to be very pleasant and helpful throughout. Would recommend going during quiet hours. We went around ten am on a Wednesday and left around noon when it started getting more crowded. Especially enjoyed experiencing the rain whilst in the warm water, with the seafront mist right in front of us.
Absolutely wonderful experience. Highly recommended.

Usually when I have 40+ cameras on me taking videos and photos of me half naked I'm on an hourly rate or at minimum getting a commission. Apparently that's not the case here.

What a weird policy; allowing complete strangers to take videos and photos of people that have paid to use your overpriced hot tub, practically in their underwear, without their permission. Not just a weird policy, but an absurdly creepy behavior by everyone doing it. I also saw 3 guys actively taking videos in the showers and changing rooms. How is this the norm?!

I'll update this review to 5 stars when phones have been banned at Sky Lagoon.

Thank you again for your honest feedback. We truly value your comments and hope to have the opportunity to provide you with a much more enjoyable experience in the future.

review:
Die Sky Lagoon ist ein aussergewöhnliches Thermalbad mit einem atemberaubenden und sehr harmonischen Design.
Die sieben Rituale sind ein absolutes Muss, und auch wenn es Überwindung kostet, sich vor der Sauna in das kalte Becken zu stürzen, ist es die Erfahrung wert.  Natürlich sollte man bei Herzkreislauferkrankungen Vorsicht walten lassen.
Der Infinity-Pool hat mich besonders beeindruckt. Außerdem gibt es zahlreiche Sitzgelegenheiten und die Anzahl der Besucher fällt bei der Grösse der Anlage kaum auf.
Zwar ist es ein teures Vergnügen, aber für uns hat es sich definitiv gelohnt!

I went to four other lagoons (all were great) - this was the most expensive, but also the venue and experience were in line with that cost.

review: Die Sky Lagoon bietet eine Mischung aus Lagune und Sauna/Spa. Es ist insgesamt alles sehr hochwertig und es wird mit den Elementen der isländischen Natur gearbeitet, also Lavastein und einer Torfhütte. Innen ist das Design „Industrial Chick“ und auch sehr stilvoll. Es lohnt sich direkt morgens um 8:00 Uhr zu kommen, denn da ist es noch sehr schön leer. Der Pool verdient wirklich den Namen Infinity mit tollem Blick auf das Meer. Das Ritual ist schön und auch sehr hochwertig: kaltes Becken, finnische Sauna mit Blick auf die Bucht, abkühlen im Regen, Salzpeeling, Dampfsauna, duschen und ein kleiner Saft. Zwischendurch gibt es Wasser kostenlos aus Wasserhähnen. Toll wäre es, wenn man das Ritual 2 mal durchlaufen könnte. Im Poolbereich gibt es leider kein Wasser, weil man vermutlich an der Bar konsumieren soll. Leider sind Smartphones überall im Pool und auch in den Saunen, wo sie nicht sein sollten (es gibt entsprechende Hinweisschilder). Das saunieren erfolgt in Badebekleidung. Insgesamt ein tolles Erlebnis wenn auch nicht ganz günstig.
response_of_owner:
Dear B. M. G.,

Thank you for taking the time to review your Sky Lagoon journey and for sharing such detailed feedback.

We're delighted to hear that you appreciated the design of Sky Lagoon, the peaceful atmosphere of an early morning visit, and the stunning views from our Infinity Lagoon. It's wonderful to know you enjoyed the Skjól Ritual and the unique elements inspired by Icelandic nature.

We also appreciate your thoughtful suggestions. While the Ritual is designed as a one-time journey during each visit, we're grateful to hear your feedback about experiencing it more than once. We'd also like to mention that complimentary drinking water is available from the faucets located around the lagoon area, as well as at the swim-up bar, and we hope this information will be helpful for your next visit.

We're sorry to hear that the use of smartphones by other guests impacted your experience. We kindly ask all guests to be considerate of others and to respect our phone-free areas, particularly within the saunas, where signage is displayed. Your comments have been shared with our team as we continue working to preserve the peaceful and relaxing atmosphere that Sky Lagoon strives to provide.

Thank you once again for your valuable feedback. We truly appreciate your insights and hope to have the pleasure of welcoming you back for another relaxing visit in the future.

review:

review: I've been to thermal pools all over the world, and Sky Lagoon is one of the nicest.  It is large enough to accommodate the crowds comfortably. The salt rub included in the ser package was a nice experience.  The water temperature was perfect.  My wife and I shared a couple of plates after the snack bar.  They were excellent!  I haven't been to the Blue Lagoon, which I assume to be a bit more commercial, but I can highly recommend the Sky Lagoon.

review:
We visited a number of community baths in Iceland, and Sky Lagoon is, of course, not one of them. It is very much its own experience. Is it kitschy? Absolutely. Swarming with tourists? No doubt. But it is also extremely well executed, clean, and remarkably comfortable.

The view over the infinity edge toward Bessastaðir and Álftanes, looking west into the sunset, is incredible. The staff are impressively friendly given the sheer volume of visitors, and the lagoon is laid out sensibly enough that it never feels especially crowded.

Expensive, certainly, but worthwhile.
response_of_owner:
Dear Joel Rosenbaum,

Thank you for taking the time to review your Sky Lagoon journey.

We're delighted to hear that you appreciated Sky Lagoon as a unique experience in its own right. While it differs from Iceland's traditional community pools, we're pleased that you found the experience to be well executed, comfortable, and impeccably maintained.

It's wonderful to know that the views across the infinity lagoon toward Bessastaðir and Álftanes left such an impression. We also truly appreciate your kind words about our team—they work hard every day to ensure every guest feels welcomed, even during our busiest times.

Thank you for recognizing the care that goes into creating a relaxing environment despite the number of visitors. We're honored that you found the experience worthwhile and hope to have the pleasure of welcoming you back the next time you're in Iceland.

Thank you again for your kind recommendation and helpful tips for future guests. We hope your next visit to Iceland brings you back to Sky Lagoon for another relaxing and memorable experience.

review:
The place is fine... until you consider the price tag (~130€ per person). For that you start to expect flawless premium experience designed with care and utmost attention to detail – Sky Lagoon fails at that:
– Buying the ticket – needed two tickets but with different pass type. So I needed to go through the checkout flow twice. And the flow did not even have Apple Pay. Not cool.
– Entering changing room – there is a sign saying "take off your shoes and put them in the locker" or something similar but there is no locker in sight. I read out that there is a dedicated shoe locker and start to look for one stepping further. Then comes the staff rudely (probably bcs he needs to say this million times per day) telling me to take shoes off right here. Ok, wow, ducking dell – you know you could just put up a sign that says "remove your shoes here and take them with you". The locker in question is the same locker where all the other stuff goes. (Seems Raja Parmar had similar experience)
– The locker – the shwippiest locker I've ever used. The door has a spring to autoclose it aggressively. So for each item you want to get in/out of the locker you got to fight with the door. And there is no shelf in the locker. My dirt-cheap gym has lockers with normal doors and shelves.
– The showers – Sér promises privacy but actually you have quite private experience with Saman also, the showers have each a booth around them so noone will see your naughty bits if you are not into that. It's super dark there so takes a bit to understand where is shower gel (or the naughty bits) and which one of the bottles it is (also in different booths they are in different order), shower start button was relatively easy to find. But what if it's cold first – I start looking up to see where the shower head is but just get blinded by a spotlight. Having no idea from where the water will hit me and with what temp I push the button – water's fine, but dude I don't want to go through this escape room experience.
– The lagoon – much warm, such view. Very instagrammable, lot of people with phones doing mostly that all the time. So If you're really into that then yeah maybe 130€ well spent. It's just warm water – not bubbles or any other feats, just the view and some rocks. Visited Landmannalaugar hot spring a few days before – free, natural, way more picturesque. Spend money on getting to Landmannalaugar instead.
– The rituals – the idea of only being able to go through the rituals once and in a specific order seemed dumb from the start (I'm from Estonia, we have a great sauna and spa culture), but I decided to give them the benefit of the doubt thinking that for that price they have probably really thought this through. Oh boy...
– Cold pool – they fail to tell you clearly on the sign that when starting with the rituals the cold pool is the first one you should take after getting out from the lagoon, so I did not go to that. After entering the ritual house door and visiting restroom a staff member started awkwardly asking if I went to the cold one. Was too cooled down already so headed to the sauna.
– The sauna – they have two saunas actually, one for instagrammers and one device free, lol. The sauna was mild, for an Estonian at least (or any other person used to real saunas, real meaning ~100 degrees celsius). A nice warm room with a nice view. Andrew Tate would think it's real hardcore manly stuff.
– The mist – nice experience, would now like to go back to sauna... nope, one way only
– The steam sauna – people using the seat cleaning showers to wash the scrub off inside sauna was disturbing, maybe have signs to educate people?
– The exit – they have three-panel revolving door with only one panel with the letters Push cut out. I got that presented to me mirrored. Understand how signs work or don't use signs guys.
To wrap it up – if I had paid 30€ I'd say "awesome", I paid 130€ and I say "disappointing".
Also my dear Sky Lagoon you now owe me for this service design consultation.
response_of_owner:
Dear Mihkel Ronk,
 
Thank you for taking the time to review your Sky Lagoon experience and for sharing such detailed feedback.
 
We are sorry to hear that your visit did not meet your expectations, especially given the premium nature of the experience. We appreciate the time you took to outline the different aspects of your visit, as feedback like this helps us identify where we can continue to improve.
 
We regret that certain parts of the journey, including the booking process, signage, changing facilities, Ritual flow, and interactions with our team, left you feeling disappointed. Your comments regarding the lockers, showers, phone use, and clarity of instructions have been shared with the relevant teams for review.
 
We understand that guests may come with different expectations, particularly when comparing Sky Lagoon with traditional Nordic spas or natural hot springs. Sky Lagoon offers a curated wellness experience, and we are sorry that it did not fully align with what you were looking for on this occasion.
 
Thank you again for your honest and comprehensive feedback. We hope that, despite this experience, you had an enjoyable trip to Iceland and wish you all the best on your travels.

14.7.26 Aivan ihana paikka. Vesi niin ihanan lämmintä sadepäivänä. Sisäänpääsy sisälsi kasvonaamion ja alkoholittoman juoman. Sää ei tosiaankaan suosinut, lonkeron harmaa päivä ja vettä satoi.

Touristy? Yes. But should you go? Absolutely! There is a REASON it's touristy. The milky blue water is one of a kind and we literally spent 5-6 hours there without getting bored. Just relaxing and enjoying the moment in the middle of a solidified lava field. What an experience! Loved the face masks and drinks too. If you don't like to see kids being kids, swim a bit further where it's deep and in a secluded place. Blue Lagoon is HUGE! You can walk for 10min and still not reaching the edge of it. So take your time and enjoy this beautiful place.

Nothing like it anywhere else. So glad I decided to go (was on the fence because of the crowds). We went at 9am and it was not crowded at all. By the time we started to shower to leave it was around 11:30 and the busses were showing up. Go early and enjoy. It's so beautiful and pleasant.
A great way to recover from a long airplane flight and kill time before our hotel check-in. It's just as beautiful as the pictures make it look and twice as relaxing

Wenn man in Island ist sollte man da hin,ist zwar ein Hot Spot der wo viele Leute sind. Vorab buchen das man sich den günstigsten Tarif aussucht,am besten vormittags gleich. Ist nicht günstig aber man hat viel was machen kann.
Så häftigt, avkopplande och helt underbart. Var där efter 19 så de flesta buss sällskap hade lämnat. Detta är verkligen något utöver det vanliga.
A place where you can truly rest and relax. Although it's expensive, it's well worth it. The facilities are excellent, and the staff are very helpful.
Insta werbe Show. Für 145€ pro Person.

世界有数の温泉スパ施設の誉れ高きblue lagoon最高でした。朝イチのin water massageは、入り口から一番遠いセクションで行われるとのことで、颯爽と、バスローブをかけて、ラグーンを横切りました。が、途中、結構深いところもあり、また、熱いお湯の噴出しているところもあり、それならばと脇の岩のヘリを進んだら、こんどは、ツルツル滑ったりで、結構楽しみました。もちろんスマホなんて、持って行きようもなく。トリートメントは、retreatホテルの方の区分で行われました。ふかふかのゴムのマットと、エア枕の上に横になります。シリカのせいで髪がガチガチになるので、コンディショナーをたくさん髪に塗りなさい、と言われますが、そんなものはなかったかのように、カチカチになります。が、丁寧に普段のジャンプートリートメントをしているうちに解決しますのでご安心ください。数少なな私の世界スパ紀行のうちでも、希少な経験でした。外気温が冬と違ってそんなに低くないので、温泉内も、そんなに高温とは違いますが、思ったより高いなあというのが印象です。その割にのぼせてしんどくはならないのが露天ということなのかも。気持ち良くて、あっさり寝てしまっで、起こしてくださり、よかったらまだこの上で寝ていますか、と聞いてくださります。都合で私はきり上げましたが、とてもリラックスできました。シャワールームには、blu lagoon印のシャンプー、ボディソープ、コンディショナーが備えられていますので、心置きなく、シリカを落としていけます。バングルに、ロッカーキー、飲食などの代金も仕込まれ、すべてチェックアウトの際に計算できます。ほんとに素晴らしい。絶対、前を通り過ぎず、立ち寄るべきところです🙌
One of the many highlights of a vacation in Iceland. Looking forward to my return.

Esperienza da fare se sì passa per reykiavik. Acqua geotermale ed una grande organizzazione hanno reso questa spa famosa. Nonostante il grande afflusso di persone si può godere delle acque calde e della pace di questo luogo scavato nella lava.
Awesome experience! Worth to try once at least.  Went early on a weekday and only started getting crowded around mid day.
too expensive for what it offers
Amazing!
Nice place to relax.
An must do whilst visiting Iceland.

Приехали с группой 25 человек из Израиля. Сначала после того, как вы входите в систему с входным браслетом, вы кладете свою одежду в шкафчик, который закрывается кодовым браслетом. Перед входом в лагуну необходимо принять душ. Виды вокруг действительно впечатляют. В некоторых местах вода теплая, а в других – горячая. Очень расслабляет. Есть бар, где подают напитки. Ваш браслет дает вам право на один алкогольный или безалкогольный напиток. Ваш браслет также дает вам право получить белую крем-маску, которую можно нанести на лицо на 10 минут. Если вы хотите зеленую или черную маску или еще напитки, вам придётся доплатить. Мы прекрасно провели время, не хотелось уходить. жаль, были ограничены во времени...
Magnifique endroit , non loin du Volcan. On se sent sur la planète Mars. Eau chaude, bar dans l'eau, sauna dans l'eau. À voir absolument
Una experiencia que no pueden perder.expectacular
We loved our experience here! We booked for a time slot in the morning during a layover and had an incredible experience. Such a relaxing and memorable place.
Well worth the price of admission. I've not felt that relaxed in years.
Fantastic experience! It's one of the must see places of Iceland even though it's expensive.
Hyggeligt sted at være. Dejligt varmt vand.
Great experience. A little pricey. Recommend if you or a loved one has psoriasis. Really helps skin issues.
מקום נפלא מאורגן ומסודר...חניה שירות נקיון למופת ...המים נהדרים

Honestly worth the price since everything in Iceland is pretty pricey anyway, and if you only get to visit the country probably once in your life, you should definitely make a stop here! We went on a weekday around noon and it’s definitely not as crowded as people say it is. The water is beautiful, comforting, and the service is amazing. I would recommend to visit right after your road trips before you fly back - it’s a wonderful cherry on top :)
питомца отвез в бассейн
Příjemné koupání, ale moc lidí😢

Do Sky Lagoon instead of this overrated place for a true spa experience.

I was very disappointed expecting a spa experience with all the marketing around that, but this is more like a public pool that happens to be scenic. The entry lines are long, like a theme park, the reception run by 18-20 year olds who don’t know customer service. There’s a long (uncovered) way to walk to the front, which if it’s cold, rainy and windy (it usually is) is quite miserable.

Figuring out how to get a robe was a whole ordeal, not knowing that wasn’t part of our ticket. Trying to leave for the restaurant and come back for our things was a whole ordeal, since whoever let us out wasn’t supposed to do that - only if you have robes can you go in and out without emptying your locker - again- no one told us that - except the 19 year old boy and girl saying “that’s the rule” as I then need to come back to the Lava restaurant with a big plastic bag of wet clothes.

This would all be a non issue with proper staff training and a checkout at the front entrance, instead of in the middle of everything.
Nice experience. New sauna and steam room. Get to try 3 face masks and a free drink at the pool bar. Changing room available. Do need better ventilation in locker rooms
Très bien tant dans l'équipement que les bains. Seul bemol, des touristes majoritairement anglophones qui ne respectent absolument pas les quiet zone.
超級舒服！全世界最舒服的溫泉，可以喝飲料、敷面膜等等。

Sehr schöne Salzwasserlagune, in der man sich gut entspannen kann. Preise sind wie erwartet sehr hoch, aber gerechtfertigt. Voranmeldung ist unbedingt nötig. Das hohe Besucheraufkommen wird vom Personal gut aufgefangen, sodass die Wartezeiten sehr gering sind. In der Lagune verteilen sich die Menschen, da die Lagune recht groß und verwinkelt ist, sodass man sich trotzdem gut erholen kann.
Märchenhaft und wundervoll.
A unique spa experience in a natural lagoon

This place is a bit gimmicky and extremely overpriced. There are many hot springs and pools on the island, and this one charges a premium for their 'healing' light blue waters. Sure, it makes a nice selfie if that's all you're in it for - but that's about it.

If it was cheaper, I'd give it a better rating. But there are other places in Iceland for half the price (or less) which are just as nice, if not better. If you're traveling around for a few days, make sure to research some of your other options first, before deciding to take the plunge on this one.

You can visit Reykjavik, Iceland without visiting the Blue Lagoon, but why would you?  You sign in, take a shower and get into the lagoon.  A free drink and face masks are included in the price of admission.  The day we went, the weather was blustery, and about 44°F.  Still, the water was quite warm and inviting.
Ez a hely valóban minden képzeletet felülmúl az árakon viszont nagyon meglepődtünk. Ugyan ki vannak írva de amikor a három tagú családnak kifizettük az azért nem esett jól de a hely nagyon szép!

Honestly, it's pretty cool, although it's not exactly the wild, untouched experience you might imagine, and it's damn expensive even compared to Icelandic prices, so don't plan your big vacation souvenir shopping spree here.
I have to admit, if I hadn't gone inside, I would probably still be daydreaming about what it was like. So you have to see it at least once, just to realize that it's not actually as extraordinary as you imagined — but it's still worth putting into the safe "things I've experienced and can stop wondering about" storage of your mind.
Bucket list fulfilled!
If you've ever wanted to take a bath with a thousand strangers, this is your mecca.

Lugar emblemático de Islandia donde poder relajarte y disfrutar de sus aguas termales naturales en un entorno volcánico ideal. Con la entrada está incluida una bebida sin alcohol, mascarilla facial, toalla, champú, gel y acondicionador de pelo.
Lo recomiendo porque el contraste entre el agua caliente y el frío de afuera es espectacular! Si volvemos a Islandia seguro que volvemos.
sortie sympathique, jusqu'à ce que je m'aperçoive du vol de mes papiers d'identité, carte vitale, argent espèce, chèques.....Quelle déception.
Highlight of the trip. Went straight from the airport. Great place to decompress and get ready for the travelling ahead.
One of the best experiences ever. Do not miss it if you are in Iceland

Ich denke hier kann ich mich kurz fassen:
Einzigartiges Erlebnis und nehmt das unbedingt mit wenn ihr auf Island seid!
舉世聞名，可以敷臉、飲料喝還有蒸汽浴，超棒的！
Super, najlepiej wcześnie rano (mało ludzi). Polska obsługa, wszyscy bardzo grzeczni. Maseczki rzeczywiście działają 😁

Recommended to me by locals who referred to it as the splooge lagoon. Highly recommended for all who want a chudsworthy vacation. The mystical sensations felt by my feet waddling through the goupy water had me truly wondering what on earth was I touching. Highly fitting for the self proclaimed title of 25th wonder of the world.

Die Lagune an sich ist nicht schlecht. SEHR touristisch und SEHR teuer. Für alle, die nur ein paar Tage rund um den Flughafen und Reykjavik verbringen, lohnt sich ein Aufenthalt. Alle die auf der Ringstraße und dem Rest der Insel unterwegs sind, empfehle ich eine der diversen weiteren Thermen auf der Insel.

It is an incredible experience. I understand they want to make money, but it felt very much like they wanted to extract as much money from you as possible. My recommendation would be to buy the minimum ticket you can and bring your own food.

Don't look at anything in the gift shop. I bought 2 t-shirts (literally 10-20$ at any survivor place in the rest of the world), a mug (maybe $20 anywhere else in the world), and some 'Blue lagoon mineral intensive cream' 300ml (I guess you can't get this anywhere else in the world, but $50 would be pretty expensive in my mind). So $110, maybe $150, right? Nope. $250!!! Insane.

They did give my son his lunch for free because I paid for a lava lunch package. It was some great food, and giving him the lunch for free kept the review from dropping to 2 stars.

I'm not sure I can recommend this place, or even Iceland as a whole. The airport has a whole huge "exit through the gift shop" nonsense also.

I'm glad I experienced it, but I didn't think I'd ever go back.

Saying from the whole,it’s worth having a try.Cause at least you arrive in the Iceland.It will take you a little bit of time to get there.If you plan to have a trip in winter or the temperature is under 0c,you would have a better experience.
Relaxing and unique.
Relaxing and unique.

This is obviously a hot tourist destination—pun intended! And for good reason. We were amazed by the size of this place and thoroughly enjoyed our entire experience.
The natural hot springs were incredible, and being able to relax and soak for hours was such a unique and refreshing experience. The included mud mask and drinks were a fun bonus that made the visit feel even more special.
The facility itself was absolutely sparkling clean, and the attention to detail really stood out. The locker rooms and showers were well maintained and stocked with their own brand of toiletries, which smelled amazing and added an extra touch of luxury.
After spending hours soaking in the warm waters, we left feeling completely refreshed and rejuvenated. While it is definitely a popular tourist attraction, we felt it was well worth the visit and would highly recommend experiencing it at least once.

藍湖(Blue Lagoon ）可以說是冰島經典不敗的指標性景點，雖然近年還有新開的天空之湖（Sky Lagoon ），但對於初訪冰島的旅客應該還是把藍湖作為必去的景點！

相較於雷克雅維克（Reykjavik)市區的車程45分鐘，藍湖離KEF機場車程20分鐘更近，時間允許的話，可以考慮作為行程第一天抵達KEF機場後或最後一天前往機場前的安排，或者安排為雷克雅維克周遭順遊的點。行程安排上至少抓2～3小時。

出入池子可以從側邊室內的水道（如影片），可以減少身體溼吹風冷。溫泉的水溫算是蠻剛好的，即使對小孩來說感受不會太燙，也不會有冷的問題。
Relaxing and unique
Recherche trop cher pour passer quelques moments 460 € pour 3

Enjoyed the experience but overall found it way too crowded. The facial bar was like a feeding frenzy and there wasn't enough fresh water spigots for the amount of people in the area. Locker rooms were cramped with not enough toilets since many women were using it to change in for privacy. Bonus is how convenient it can be when traveling to/from the airport.
Beautiful church!!!
Tolle Architektur, innen relativ schlicht gehalten.  Gut fand ich den Aufzug zum Glockenturm
雷克雅維克最震撼人心的精神地標！建築靈感取自冰島獨特的玄武岩柱地形，宏偉挺拔的外觀無論從市區哪個角度看都極具存在感。搭乘電梯登上頂樓鐘塔，能 360 度俯瞰整座首都繽紛可愛的彩色屋頂與遠方峽灣交織的絕美全景。內部簡約莊嚴，巨大的管風琴令人讚嘆，是初訪冰島絕不可錯過的經典一站！2017.05.30

Very impressive structure, especially when you stand at the doorway and just look up. Fabulous organ inside, unfortunately didn't get a chance to hear it play. Highly recommend going up the bell tower,  stunning views of the city. The bell chimes every 15 minutes and can catch you off guard.

Jeden z symboli Islandii. Jest to kościuł luterański. W środku ma wygląd surowy. Wejście na wieżę kościelna jest płatne, ale samo wejście do kościoła nie jest. Jest tam sklep z pamiątkami. Parkingu w pobliżu są płatne.

The church has a cool modern architecture but that's pretty much it as it's not as grand or magestic. We didn't try the elevator to the top though. Maybe that can add some value (of course you need to pay the elevator fee). It was cool to see the statue of Leif Erikson.
Impressive church in Reykjavik
Wunderschöe Kirche, zentral gelegen.
Attrazione unica. Bellissima anche la torre da cui si vede tutta la città
jolie en dedans et à l 'extérieur. Mais c'est froid comme ambiance...pas de vitraux ni couleur. Une église sans accès aux matériaux en bois.
Qu'elle est haute et deroutante ! On ne sait quoi en penser. Mais,  elle a ce côté unique qu'on aime tant. Du jamais vu pour ma part.

Very cool church! Can see why it’s so popular. I love that the entrance is free, and I think it’s fair that they charge you to go up.

Vom Turm hat man einen schönen Überblick über ganz Reykjavik.

Die Besichtigung der Kieche ist kostenlos, es fallen maximal Kosten für den Kirchturm an. Der Ticketverkauf für den Turm findet im kleinen Shop, wenn man rein kommt links, statt, allerdings hat am Fahrzug selbst niemand das Ticket sehen wollen.

iconic landmark - you can’t miss it!

C'est une belle église iconique de Reykjavik. Ne payez pas pour y monter si vous comptez aller au musée Perlan ou la vue est beaucoup plus impressionnante!
Wasn’t able to go inside as I was around after 8pm. However it is very beautiful, love the architecture.
Best in town.

Its a nice big church from the outside but quite boring inside. It's very big but if you're looking for nice, old fashioned stained glass windows, forget it. It is a fairly new church so it's not really in that genre. The organ is impressive though. We didn't do the view from the top as the queue was a bit too long. Little gift shop was nice though but just like the rest of Iceland, pricey
We arrived in time for the 11am service and we welcomed

Obviously a very popular tourist location. It was super busy at 11am when we visited. Despite the high volume of people, you could still walk around inside and admire the building. There was quite a queue for the lift to the top of the church, so we hope to do this if we visit in future.
A very beautiful and “unique” church, both inside and out. It's worth a visit.

Sehr beeindruckender Bau. Mit einem Lift kommt man auf den Turm und dann sind es nur 33 Treppenstufen auf die Plattform. Es gibt eine schöne Aussicht auf die Stadt und der Umgebung.
